2015-08-25.abstract: What began with complaints about the government’s inability to clean the streets escalated to calls for an overhaul of the sectarian-based political system.

2015-08-25.title: In Syria, Potential Ally’s Islamist Ties Challenge U.S.
2015-08-25.abstract: Ahrar al-Sham opposes the Syrian president and the Islamic State and seems like a logical Western ally, but its links to Al Qaeda trouble American policy makers.

2015-08-26.title: Vultures Face Own Death
2015-08-26.abstract: Across East Africa, vulture populations have been decimated by poaching, poisoning and other human interventions.

2015-08-26.title: Water Is Called Casualty of Syrian War
2015-08-26.abstract: In a report about the scarcity of water in Syria, Unicef said it had recorded 18 deliberate cuts to the public water supply in Aleppo this year.

2015-08-26.title: Iran: Official Denies Any Plan to Trade U.S. Reporter
2015-08-26.abstract: Iran has no plans to trade Jason Rezaian, the imprisoned Washington Post correspondent, for any Iranian citizens held by the United States.

2015-08-26.title: U.N. Report Details Flow of Foreign Weapons Into South Sudan
2015-08-26.abstract: The study came as the Security Council considered a draft resolution that would impose an arms embargo on the country.

2015-08-26.title: China: Legal Ordeals for Brothers of a Journalist
2015-08-26.abstract: A court in China’s western region of Xinjiang tried two brothers of an American reporter on charges of endangering state security and leaking secrets.

2015-08-27.title: U.N. Says Aid Increase Would Cut Migration
2015-08-27.abstract: The under secretary general of humanitarian affairs, fresh from visits to embattled nations, urged affluent nations to step up aid as a way to stem the flow of refugees.

2015-08-27.title: Saudi Arabia: King Plans to Visit U.S.
2015-08-27.abstract: King Salman will visit the White House next week for the first time since ascending to the throne, Saudi officials said.

2015-08-27.title: Hong Kong Christian Groups Feel New Scrutiny From Mainland
2015-08-27.abstract: An effort by Chinese officials to limit the influence of religion has raised concerns about encroachment on the city’s autonomy.

2015-08-27.title: Palestinians Want to Raise Flag at U.N.
2015-08-27.abstract: The General Assembly received a draft resolution calling for the organization’s two “nonmember observer states” to be granted the right to raise their flags.

2015-08-27.title: China Holds 23 Linked to Fatal Blasts in Tianjin
2015-08-27.abstract: Chinese leaders have moved vigorously in recent days to quell outrage over the blasts, which rattled top leaders and raised questions about the safety of China’s industrial zones.

2015-08-28.title: Rights Groups Stirring Over Sudan President’s Travels
2015-08-28.abstract: Omar al-Bashir has eluded prosecution on international charges of war crimes in Darfur, but two scheduled trips are drawing new attention to his case.

2015-08-28.title: Sit-In Leaders Are Charged in Hong Kong
2015-08-28.abstract: Three students who led last year’s pro-democracy Umbrella Movement in Hong Kong were charged over their roles in setting off the massive rallies and sit-ins.

2015-08-28.title: Lawyer Who Represented Churches in China Is Missing
2015-08-28.abstract: Zhang Kai and his assistant seemed to have disappeared on Tuesday night in Wenzhou, where they had gone to advise Protestant churches about the government’s recent demolition of crosses and church buildings.

2015-08-28.title: Kim Jong-un Says North’s ‘Military Muscle’ Made Korean Deal Possible
2015-08-28.abstract: The agreement “was by no means something achieved on the negotiating table,” North Korea’s official news media quoted the country’s leader as saying.

2015-08-28.title: Europe Reels From More Migrant Deaths on Land and Sea
2015-08-28.abstract: Deaths of about 150 people at sea near Libya and 71 found in a truck in Austria came as the United Nations reported a surge in 2015 migrations.

2015-08-29.title: Countries Under the Most Strain in the European Migration Crisis
2015-08-29.abstract: European Union officials struggle to cope with the growing crisis.

2015-08-29.title: Now Playing Around the World, the ‘Trump Show’
2015-08-29.abstract: Donald Trump is derided as “a nutcase” in France and “a racist” in China, and his anti-immigrant rhetoric baffles Germany, where his grandparents were born.

2015-08-29.title: Grim Findings in Austria and at Sea Push Migrant Crisis Into Public View
2015-08-29.abstract: The discovery of 71 bodies in a truck in Austria, and the drowning of more than 150 people off the Libyan coast have led to new calls to focus on human trafficking.

2015-08-29.title: U.N. Urges the Netherlands to Stop Portrayals of ‘Black Pete’ Character
2015-08-29.abstract: After a report said the character promoted racial stereotyping, the Dutch government said it would encourage discussion about racism, but dismissed the idea of a ban.

2015-08-29.title: Antigovernment Protesters Gather in Malaysia, Defying Police Orders
2015-08-29.abstract: Thousands massed in Kuala Lumpur in a display of anger at the government of Prime Minister Najib Razak, who has been accused of corruption.

2015-08-30.title: Met Extends Show on China’s Fashion Influence
2015-08-30.abstract: The Metropolitan Museum of Art’s “China: Through the Looking Glass” is attaining blockbuster status, Vanessa Friedman writes. It’s proven so popular, including with Chinese visitors, that it will be extended through Sept. 7, Randy Kennedy reports.

2015-08-30.title: Behind Deadly Tianjin Blast, Shortcuts and Lax Rules
2015-08-30.abstract: Rui Hai International Logistics, whose warehouses were hit by the deadly explosions, has become a symbol of the high cost of rapid industrialization in a closed political system rife with graft.

2015-08-30.title: Tianjin Explosion Reveals Company That Exploited Weak Governance
2015-08-30.abstract: More than two weeks after explosions at its warehouses killed 145 people, Rui Hai International Logistics has become a symbol of the high cost of rapid industrialization in a closed political system rife with corruption.

2015-08-30.title: Jason Rezaian’s Status Unclear After Iran Sentences 2
2015-08-30.abstract: The judiciary did not release the names of the two people convicted of spying for the United States and Israel.

2015-08-30.title: 1965: Dr. King’s Children Enroll in White School
2015-08-30.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Dr. Martin Luther King enrolled two of his children in a formerly all-white school in 1965.

2015-08-31.title: ISIS Damages Temple of Baal in Palmyra
2015-08-31.abstract: The Syrian Observatory for Human Rights said that fighters had destroyed part of the nearly 2,000-year-old temple.

2015-08-31.title: Indian Scholar Who Criticized Worship of Idols Is Killed
2015-08-31.abstract: The killing of the scholar, Malleshappa Madivalappa Kalburgi, drew immediate comparisons to the 2013 murder of Dr. Narendra Dabholkar, who spent decades debunking gurus, sorcerers, healers and godmen.

2015-08-31.title: Growing Migrant Crisis Prompts Call for Urgent Meeting of E.U. Officials
2015-08-31.abstract: Germany, France and Britain appealed for the nations to establish specific measures for helping people who flee to the continent, and the European Union’s leadership set a meeting for Sept. 14.

2015-08-31.title: Caste Quotas in India Come Under Attack
2015-08-31.abstract: Resentment has gelled into opposition to India’s version of affirmative action, a system of strict quotas that reserves nearly half of jobs for those from disadvantaged castes or tribes.

2015-08-31.title: At Least 13 Reported Dead in Yemen Strikes
2015-08-31.abstract: The Saudi-led military coalition killed workers at a water plant on Sunday, the plant’s owner said, while a coalition spokesman said Houthi rebels had made explosive devices at the site.
